disclaimer: Little Leaps is a fun, educational tool — not a medical screening or diagnosis. It's based on CDC developmental guidelines and designed to help you celebrate your child's growth milestones. Every child's journey is beautifully unique. You know your child best. If you ever have questions — even if every box is checked — our team is always here for a free, friendly conversation. This tool does not create a therapist-client relationship. Always discuss your child's development with your pediatrician or a qualified professional. Milestone data: CDC "Learn the Signs. Act Early." (2022 revision). Activities informed by ASHA, AOTA, APTA, and evidence-based ABA practices.
disclaimerShort: This is an educational tool, not a diagnosis. Every child is unique!

soaring.label: Soaring!
soaring.message: Your little one is soaring! 🎉 So many amazing milestones happening. Keep celebrating every moment — and remember, we're always here if you have questions about their journey.

growing.label: Growing Strong
growing.message: Your child is growing strong in so many areas! 💪 A few milestones are still on their way — and that's totally normal. If you'd like to explore these areas together, a free 15-minute check-in is a great next step.

blooming.label: Room to Bloom
blooming.message: Every child blooms in their own time 🌻 Some milestones are still unfolding, and early exploration can unlock incredible growth. Our free screening is a friendly, no-pressure conversation about how we can help your little one thrive.

alwaysMessage: You know your child best. Trust your instincts — if anything feels worth exploring, we're always here.

screeningCTA.subtext: Free, friendly, no obligation — just a conversation about your child's journey

speech.text: Makes sounds other than crying

activities.title: Talk & Pause
activities.desc: Talk to your baby in a sing-song voice, then pause. Wait for them to make a sound back — any sound counts!

activities.title: Sound Play
activities.desc: Make gentle sounds — coo, hum, click your tongue. Watch for their mouth to move or a small sound to come out.

speech.text: Reacts to loud sounds

activities.title: Clap & Watch
activities.desc: Clap your hands gently near (not too close to) your baby. Watch for a startle, blink, or head turn.

activities.title: Rattle Shake
activities.desc: Shake a rattle or crinkle paper to one side. See if they turn toward the sound. Try the other side!

speech.text: Makes cooing sounds when content

activities.title: Coo Conversation
activities.desc: When your baby coos, coo back! Mirror their sounds and add new ones. It's their first 'conversation.'

activities.title: Calm Narration
activities.desc: During calm alert times, narrate what you see: 'You're so happy! I see your little smile!' They start cooing along.

speech.text: Quiets or smiles when spoken to

activities.title: Face-to-Face Chat
activities.desc: Hold your face 8-12 inches from theirs. Talk softly and smile. Watch for them to quiet, focus, or smile back.

activities.title: Singing Calm
activities.desc: When they're fussy, try singing softly. A familiar tune helps them learn to calm when they hear your voice.

speech.text: Cries differently for different needs (hunger, tired, uncomfortable)

activities.title: Cry Detective
activities.desc: Start noticing patterns — a hungry cry may sound different from a tired cry. Responding to each one teaches them communication works!

activities.title: Narrate the Need
activities.desc: When you figure out what they need, say it: 'Oh, you're hungry! Let's eat!' This labels their communication.

speech.text: Turns head toward familiar voices

activities.title: Voice Locate
activities.desc: Have a family member talk from different spots in the room. Watch if baby turns toward the familiar voice!

activities.title: Parent Voices
activities.desc: Take turns talking — one parent on each side. Baby is learning to find and prefer familiar voices.

physical.text: Holds head up when on tummy

activities.title: Tummy Time Face
activities.desc: Lay them on their tummy and get on the floor at their eye level. Smile and talk — they'll lift that head to see you!

activities.title: Chest Tummy Time
activities.desc: Recline on the couch and lay baby on your chest, tummy down. They'll push up to look at your face!

physical.text: Moves both arms and both legs

activities.title: Bicycle Legs
activities.desc: Gently cycle their legs while they lie on their back. Sing a song while you do it — they love the rhythm!

activities.title: Arm Stretches
activities.desc: Gently stretch their arms out wide, then cross them over their chest. Do it slowly and rhythmically.

physical.text: Briefly opens hands

activities.title: Finger Touch
activities.desc: Gently stroke the back of their hand from wrist to fingertip. This encourages them to open their fist.

activities.title: Palm Play
activities.desc: Place a soft cloth or your finger in their palm. They'll grip and release — both are great practice!

physical.text: Can hold head up briefly when held upright

activities.title: Upright Cuddle
activities.desc: Hold baby upright against your shoulder. They'll practice lifting their head to look around. Support their neck!

activities.title: Mirror Look
activities.desc: Hold them upright in front of a mirror. They'll try to lift their head to see the interesting face!

physical.text: Pushes up slightly during tummy time

activities.title: Tummy Time Prop
activities.desc: Roll up a small towel and place it under their chest during tummy time. This gives a little boost to help them push up!

activities.title: Toy Motivation
activities.desc: Place a colorful toy just in front of them during tummy time. They'll try to push up to see it better!

physical.text: Makes smoother movements with arms and legs

activities.title: Gentle Massage
activities.desc: Gently massage their arms and legs with smooth strokes. This helps body awareness and encourages controlled movement.

activities.title: Kick Time
activities.desc: During diaper changes, let them kick freely. Smile and narrate: 'Look at those kicks!' Free movement builds strength.

cognitive.text: Watches you as you move

activities.title: Slow Dance
activities.desc: Move slowly from side to side while facing your baby. Watch their eyes follow you — that's tracking!

activities.title: Peek Around
activities.desc: Move to different spots around them and call their name. See if their eyes search for you.

cognitive.text: Looks at a toy for several seconds

activities.title: High Contrast Cards
activities.desc: Hold a black-and-white image 8-12 inches from their face. Babies this age love high contrast patterns!

activities.title: Colorful Toy Hold
activities.desc: Hold a bright toy steady in front of them. Give them time to focus — even 5-10 seconds is great!

cognitive.text: Follows objects with eyes side to side

activities.title: Slow Toy Tracking
activities.desc: Hold a bright toy in front of their face and slowly move it side to side. Go slowly — let their eyes catch up!

activities.title: Face Follow
activities.desc: Move your face slowly from one side to the other while talking. Your face is the best thing to track!

cognitive.text: Shows interest in faces

activities.title: Face Time
activities.desc: During alert times, hold your face close and make different expressions — smile, open mouth wide, stick out tongue.

activities.title: Family Faces
activities.desc: Show them photos of family members' faces on your phone. Hold still and let them study each face.

cognitive.text: Begins to pay attention to faces more than objects

activities.title: Face Study Time
activities.desc: Hold baby close and let them study your face. Make slow expressions — blink, smile, open mouth. They're learning faces!

activities.title: Face vs. Toy
activities.desc: Hold a toy in one hand and your face nearby. Notice which one they look at longer — at this age, faces usually win!

cognitive.text: Begins to act bored if activity doesn't change (cries, gets fussy)

activities.title: Activity Rotation
activities.desc: If they get fussy, try changing the scene — new position, new room, or a new toy. They're telling you they need something new!

activities.title: Variety Play
activities.desc: Alternate between tummy time, back time, being held upright, and looking at toys. Short bursts keep things interesting!

social.text: Calms down when spoken to or picked up

activities.title: Voice Comfort
activities.desc: When they're fussy, try your voice first: 'I'm here, you're okay.' Sometimes sound alone can calm them.

activities.title: Swaddle & Talk
activities.desc: Swaddle snugly and talk softly. The combo of containment + your voice is very soothing at this age.

social.text: Looks at your face

activities.title: Feeding Gaze
activities.desc: During feeding, make eye contact and talk softly. This is prime bonding and face-study time.

activities.title: Close Conversations
activities.desc: Get face-to-face during calm moments. Simply being present and close encourages them to study your features.

social.text: Seems happy to see you when you walk up

activities.title: Happy Hello
activities.desc: When you approach, use a bright voice: 'Hi baby!' Watch for their body to get excited — kicking legs, widening eyes.

activities.title: Return Ritual
activities.desc: After being away (even briefly), always greet them warmly. Consistency builds their anticipation of you!

social.text: Smiles when you talk to or smile at them

activities.title: Smile Mirror
activities.desc: Smile big at your baby and wait. It may take a moment, but that first social smile is coming! Be patient.

activities.title: Silly Faces
activities.desc: Make exaggerated happy faces — big smiles, wide eyes, raised eyebrows. Babies are wired to respond to faces!

social.text: Tries to look at parent during feeding

activities.title: Feeding Connection
activities.desc: During feeding, position yourself so baby can see your face. Talk softly and make eye contact — this is bonding time!

activities.title: Eye Contact Practice
activities.desc: If bottle-feeding, hold the bottle at an angle that lets baby see your eyes. Smile when they look up at you.

social.text: Has different cries to show hunger, tiredness, or discomfort

activities.title: Respond & Label
activities.desc: When you respond to their cry, label the emotion: 'You're sleepy, I hear you.' This builds their early emotional vocabulary.

activities.title: Comfort Routine
activities.desc: Develop different comfort responses — rocking for sleepy, feeding for hungry. Consistent responses build trust and attachment.

speech.text: Makes sounds like 'oooo' and 'aahh' (cooing)

activities.title: Vowel Duets
activities.desc: When they say 'ooo,' say it back! Then try 'aah.' Take turns making vowel sounds — it's a duet!

activities.title: Sing-Song Talk
activities.desc: Use an exaggerated sing-song voice when talking. Higher pitch and longer vowels encourage their own sound-making.

speech.text: Makes sounds back when you talk to them

activities.title: Chat & Wait
activities.desc: Say something short, then WAIT 5-10 seconds. Give them time to respond. The pause is the magic part!

activities.title: Echo Game
activities.desc: Copy every sound they make. When they realize you're echoing them, they'll make more sounds to test you!

speech.text: Turns head toward the sound of your voice

activities.title: Name Game
activities.desc: Stand to one side and say their name. When they turn toward you, smile big! Try the other side.

activities.title: Where's the Sound?
activities.desc: Use a rattle or bell and make sound from different positions — left, right, above. Watch for the head turn!

speech.text: Begins to babble with expression

activities.title: Babble Back
activities.desc: When they babble with emotion, respond as if they said something meaningful: 'Oh really? Tell me more!' Keeps them going.

activities.title: Emotion Narration
activities.desc: Match your tone to theirs: when they sound excited, get excited too. When they're calm, respond calmly.

speech.text: Babbles with expression and copies sounds they hear

activities.title: Sound Copy Cat
activities.desc: Make a simple sound — 'ba ba' or 'ma ma' — and wait. If they try to copy, celebrate! If not, try again later.

activities.title: Expression Mimicry
activities.desc: Use exaggerated expressions when talking — surprise, excitement, whispers. They'll start babbling with emotion too!

speech.text: Cries in different ways to show different needs

activities.title: Need Narration
activities.desc: When you figure out what they need, narrate it: 'Oh, you're telling me you're hungry!' This validates their communication.

activities.title: Response Time
activities.desc: Respond consistently to their different cries. Quick, warm responses teach them that communicating works!

physical.text: Holds head steady without support

activities.title: Sit-Up Practice
activities.desc: Hold them in a seated position on your lap. Let them practice holding their head steady while you support their trunk.

activities.title: Look Around Time
activities.desc: Hold them upright and slowly walk around the room. The interesting visuals motivate them to hold their head up!

physical.text: Holds a toy when you put it in their hand

activities.title: Rattle Grasp
activities.desc: Place a lightweight rattle in their hand and shake it gently. They'll hold on and begin to connect 'hold = sound!'

activities.title: Fabric Hold
activities.desc: Offer a small, soft piece of fabric. They'll grip and explore the texture. Different fabrics = new sensations!

physical.text: Uses arms to swing at toys

activities.title: Toy Dangle
activities.desc: Hold a toy above them while they're on their back. Dangle it within arm's reach and cheer when they swipe!

activities.title: Kick & Bat
activities.desc: Hang light toys from a play gym or hold them overhead. Encourage batting with arms and kicking with feet!

physical.text: Brings hands to mouth

activities.title: Hand Discovery
activities.desc: During calm times, gently guide their hand to their mouth. They're learning about their own body!

activities.title: Teether Play
activities.desc: Offer a clean teether. They'll bring it to their mouth — building hand-to-mouth coordination!

physical.text: Pushes up onto elbows when on tummy

activities.title: Tummy Time Mirror
activities.desc: Place a baby-safe mirror in front during tummy time. They'll push up on elbows to see that interesting face!

activities.title: Rolled Towel Boost
activities.desc: Roll a small towel under their chest during tummy time. It gives a boost to make pushing up easier.

physical.text: Brings hands to mouth

activities.title: Hand Discovery
activities.desc: Let them explore their hands naturally. If they bring hands to their mouth, that's great self-discovery and coordination!

activities.title: Teether Play
activities.desc: Offer a soft teether — bringing it to their mouth is great hand-mouth coordination practice!

cognitive.text: Opens mouth when they see breast or bottle (if hungry)

activities.title: Feeding Cues Chat
activities.desc: Before feeding, show the bottle/breast and narrate: 'Are you hungry? Here comes milk!' They learn to anticipate.

activities.title: Routine Words
activities.desc: Use the same words before each feeding. 'Time to eat!' Repetition builds their connection between words and events.

cognitive.text: Looks at their hands with interest

activities.title: Hand Show
activities.desc: Gently hold their hands in front of their face. Wiggle their fingers. They're fascinated by their own hands!

activities.title: Colorful Mittens
activities.desc: Put a bright sock on their hand (like a mitten). They'll stare and wave it around — discovering they control it!

cognitive.text: Follows a moving toy with eyes from side to side

activities.title: Arc Tracking
activities.desc: Move a bright toy in a slow arc from left to right. Go at their pace — smooth, even movements work best.

activities.title: Bubble Watch
activities.desc: Blow bubbles above them. Their eyes will follow the floating bubbles across their field of vision!

cognitive.text: Recognizes familiar objects like a bottle

activities.title: Show & Name
activities.desc: Show familiar objects throughout the day and name them: 'Here's your bottle! Here's your blankie!' Repetition builds recognition.

activities.title: Object Parade
activities.desc: Hold up familiar items one at a time. Watch for signs of recognition — excited kicks, reaching, or sounds!

cognitive.text: Responds to affection (cuddles back, smiles)

activities.title: Cuddle Conversations
activities.desc: During snuggle time, notice if they mold into you, relax, or smile. That's their way of showing they feel safe!

activities.title: Gentle Touch
activities.desc: Stroke their cheek or rub their back. Watch for them to lean in or calm — they're responding to your affection.

cognitive.text: Reaches for a toy with one hand

activities.title: Just Out of Reach
activities.desc: Hold a toy just barely within reach. Give them time to try to grab it — even a swipe counts as reaching!

activities.title: Dangling Toys
activities.desc: Use a play gym or dangle a toy above them. They'll start batting and then progress to reaching and grasping!

social.text: Smiles on their own to get your attention

activities.title: Smile & Respond
activities.desc: When they smile at you, ALWAYS respond with a big smile and words: 'You're smiling! I love it!' This reinforces social smiling.

activities.title: Surprise Face
activities.desc: Make a surprised face when they smile. The reaction encourages them to smile more to see what you'll do!

social.text: Chuckles (not yet a full laugh)

activities.title: Gentle Tickle
activities.desc: Light tickles on their tummy or under their chin. The anticipation before the tickle is what builds the chuckle!

activities.title: Funny Sounds
activities.desc: Make silly sounds — raspberries, popping lips, funny faces. Watch for that first chuckle!

social.text: Looks at you, moves, or makes sounds to get attention

activities.title: Respond to Bids
activities.desc: When they look at you and wiggle or vocalize, respond immediately. They're learning: 'When I do this, they notice!'

activities.title: Narrate Their Moves
activities.desc: When they kick or vocalize, narrate: 'You're kicking! You want me to play!' This validates their communication.

social.text: Enjoys playing with people and may cry when playing stops

activities.title: Play Re-Start
activities.desc: Play peekaboo, then stop. Wait for them to signal they want more (fussing, reaching). Then restart — they learn to ask!

activities.title: Gentle Transition
activities.desc: When play ends, transition with a song or gentle narration: 'All done playing! Let's go see...' instead of abrupt stops.

social.text: Laughs out loud

activities.title: Silly Surprise
activities.desc: Try gentle tickles, peek-a-boo, or funny noises. That first belly laugh is magical — keep experimenting!

activities.title: Sound Surprise
activities.desc: Make unexpected but gentle sounds — a pop with your lips, a raspberry. Surprise is a great laugh trigger!

social.text: Shows excitement by waving arms and kicking legs

activities.title: Excitement Mirror
activities.desc: When they kick and wave excitedly, match their energy: 'You're so excited!' This validates their emotions.

activities.title: Favorite Toy Reveal
activities.desc: Hide a favorite toy behind your back, then reveal it. Watch for the arm-waving and leg-kicking excitement!

speech.text: Takes turns making sounds with you

activities.title: Sound Tennis
activities.desc: Make a sound, then wait. When they respond, make another sound. You're playing 'sound tennis' — back and forth!

activities.title: Whisper & Loud
activities.desc: Alternate between whispering and speaking louder. They'll try to match your volume. It teaches listening and responding.

speech.text: Blows 'raspberries' (sticks tongue out and blows)

activities.title: Raspberry Contest
activities.desc: Blow a big raspberry and wait. When they do it back, cheer! Take turns seeing who can make the silliest one.

activities.title: Lip Games
activities.desc: Make different mouth shapes and sounds — pop lips, blow air, stick tongue out. They'll experiment copying you!

speech.text: Makes squealing noises

activities.title: Squeal Along
activities.desc: When they squeal, squeal back! Match their excitement level. High-pitched sounds are excellent vocal practice.

activities.title: Tickle Squeals
activities.desc: Gentle tickles often bring out squeals! The anticipation of the tickle is the fun part.

speech.text: Begins to string vowel sounds together ('ah', 'eh', 'oh')

activities.title: Vowel Chain
activities.desc: String your own vowels together: 'ah-oh-ee!' Make it musical. They'll try to copy the pattern.

activities.title: Singing Vowels
activities.desc: Sing simple songs emphasizing the vowels. 'Row, row, row' becomes 'ooh, ooh, ooh!' Easy to copy.

speech.text: Strings vowels together when babbling ('ah,' 'eh,' 'oh')

activities.title: Vowel Strings
activities.desc: Say long vowel strings: 'ahhh-ohhh-eeeee.' Exaggerate each sound. They'll start chaining sounds together too!

activities.title: Singing Vowels
activities.desc: Sing simple songs emphasizing the vowels. 'Twinkle Twinkle' becomes 'aaah aaah ooooh ooooh' — they love it!

speech.text: Makes sounds to show joy and displeasure

activities.title: Emotion Sounds
activities.desc: When they squeal with joy, say 'You're so happy!' When they fuss, say 'You don't like that!' Label the emotion behind the sound.

activities.title: Sound Reactions
activities.desc: Play different types of sounds or music. Notice which ones get happy sounds vs. fussy sounds. They're expressing preferences!

physical.text: Rolls from tummy to back

activities.title: Toy Lure Roll
activities.desc: During tummy time, hold a toy to one side so they look and reach for it. The weight shift helps them flip!

activities.title: Gentle Hip Guide
activities.desc: Place your hand on their hip during tummy time and gently guide them to roll when they're off-balance.

physical.text: Pushes up with straight arms when on tummy

activities.title: Airplane Play
activities.desc: During tummy time, place a toy just out of reach above eye level. They'll push up higher to see it!

activities.title: Chest Support
activities.desc: Place a rolled towel or small pillow under their chest. It helps them get the feel of pushing up with arms straight.

physical.text: Leans on hands to support self when sitting

activities.title: Tripod Sit
activities.desc: Sit them between your legs with toys in front. They'll lean forward on their hands to play. You're the safety net!

activities.title: Boppy Sit
activities.desc: Place a pillow around them in a ring for support. Let them practice sitting and leaning on their hands to play.

physical.text: Rocks back and forth on hands and knees

activities.title: Rock & Sing
activities.desc: When they get on hands and knees, sing a song with a rocking beat. The rhythm encourages rocking!

activities.title: Tunnel View
activities.desc: Get on your hands and knees facing them. Rock back and forth together. They love mimicking you!

physical.text: Pushes up with straight arms during tummy time

activities.title: Airplane Tummy
activities.desc: During tummy time, hold a toy higher so they need to push up more to see it. This builds arm strength!

activities.title: Mirror Push-Up
activities.desc: Place a baby-safe mirror in front during tummy time. They'll push up higher to see that fascinating baby!

physical.text: Rocks back and forth on hands and knees

activities.title: Rock & Roll
activities.desc: If they get on hands and knees, gently rock them back and forth. Sing a rocking song to make it fun!

activities.title: Crawl Motivation
activities.desc: Place a favorite toy just ahead of them while on hands and knees. The rocking motion is pre-crawling practice!

cognitive.text: Puts things in their mouth to explore them

activities.title: Safe Taste Tour
activities.desc: Offer safe objects of different textures (rubber toy, wooden spoon, cloth). Mouthing IS how babies explore!

activities.title: Teether Variety
activities.desc: Offer different shaped teethers — bumpy, smooth, cold. Each one teaches them something new through mouthing.

cognitive.text: Reaches to grab a toy they want

activities.title: Just Out of Reach
activities.desc: Place a toy slightly beyond their reach. Encourage them to stretch for it — but don't frustrate them. Help if needed!

activities.title: Two Toy Choice
activities.desc: Hold two toys up and see which one they reach for. This practices reaching AND decision-making!

cognitive.text: Closes lips to show they don't want more food

activities.title: Respect the Signal
activities.desc: When they close their lips or turn away, say 'All done! You told me!' Respecting their signal teaches communication.

activities.title: Food Play
activities.desc: Let them explore food with their hands before eating. When they push food away, narrate: 'No thank you!'

cognitive.text: Shows curiosity about things and tries to reach them

activities.title: Discovery Basket
activities.desc: Fill a basket with safe, interesting objects (spoon, scarf, crinkle ball). Let them explore and reach into it.

activities.title: New Object Daily
activities.desc: Each day, offer one new safe item to explore (whisk, measuring cup, fabric swatch). Novelty fuels curiosity!

cognitive.text: Puts things in their mouth to explore

activities.title: Safe Texture Box
activities.desc: Offer safe items with different textures — smooth teether, bumpy ball, soft fabric. Mouthing is how they learn!

activities.title: Taste-Safe Play
activities.desc: During supervised play, let them mouth safe toys of different shapes. They're gathering information!

cognitive.text: Shows curiosity about things and tries to get at out-of-reach objects

activities.title: Reach Challenge
activities.desc: Place interesting toys just barely out of reach. Encourage them to wiggle, stretch, or roll to get it!

activities.title: Curiosity Walk
activities.desc: Carry them around and let them reach for safe objects — a leaf, a wooden spoon, a stuffed animal on a shelf.

social.text: Knows familiar people

activities.title: Who's That?
activities.desc: When familiar people arrive, name them: 'Look, it's Grandma!' Watch for excited recognition — kicking, smiling!

activities.title: Family Song
activities.desc: Make up a little tune for each family member. Sing it when they arrive. Baby will start associating songs with people!

social.text: Likes to look at self in a mirror

activities.title: Mirror Play
activities.desc: Hold them in front of a mirror and point: 'Who's that? It's YOU!' Touch their nose, then touch the mirror nose.

activities.title: Mirror Faces
activities.desc: Make silly faces in the mirror together. They'll watch both your real face and the mirror face!

social.text: Laughs

activities.title: Surprise Peekaboo
activities.desc: Play peekaboo from unexpected places — behind a door, under a blanket, around a corner. Surprise = laughs!

activities.title: Funny Noises
activities.desc: Try different silly sounds — animal noises, lip buzzing, funny voices. Find the one that gets the biggest laugh!

social.text: Responds to other people's emotions with interest

activities.title: Feelings Show
activities.desc: Show exaggerated emotions: big smile (happy!), pouty face (sad!), surprised face (wow!). Name each one.

activities.title: Empathy Model
activities.desc: When someone around them is upset, narrate: 'Daddy looks sad. Let's give him a hug!' They watch and learn.

social.text: Recognizes familiar people and knows if someone is a stranger

activities.title: Family Album
activities.desc: Show photos of family members while saying their names. Watch if they smile at familiar faces vs. look cautious at unknown ones.

activities.title: Warm Introductions
activities.desc: When meeting new people, hold baby close and let them observe. Don't force interaction — cautious is healthy!

social.text: Likes to look at self in a mirror

activities.title: Mirror Play
activities.desc: Sit with baby in front of a mirror. Point to their reflection: 'Who's that baby?' Watch for smiles and reaching.

activities.title: Mirror Faces
activities.desc: Make funny faces together in front of a mirror. They'll love watching both your face and their own!

speech.text: Makes different sounds like 'mamamama' and 'babababa'

activities.title: Babble Song
activities.desc: Make up a silly song using 'ba ba ba' or 'ma ma ma.' They'll join in with their own babble!

activities.title: Sound Menu
activities.desc: Offer different consonant sounds: 'ba ba ba... da da da... ma ma ma.' Watch which ones they try to copy!

speech.text: Responds to their own name when called

activities.title: Name Calling Game
activities.desc: Say their name from different spots in the room. When they look, smile big and say 'There you are!'

activities.title: Name + Action
activities.desc: Pair their name with simple actions: 'Maya, look!' 'Maya, clap!' They learn their name comes before something fun.

speech.text: Makes sounds to show joy or displeasure

activities.title: Emotion Echo
activities.desc: When they squeal with joy, squeal back! When they fuss, narrate: 'You sound frustrated. I hear you.' Mirror their emotions.

activities.title: Celebrate Sounds
activities.desc: Any time they vocalize their feelings, respond: 'You're so happy!' or 'I know, that's tricky.' They learn sounds = communication.

speech.text: Understands 'no' and basic tone changes

activities.title: Tone Play
activities.desc: Use different tones throughout the day: excited voice for play, calm voice for rest, firm voice for 'no.' They learn to read your tone.

activities.title: Redirect Game
activities.desc: When you say 'no,' immediately redirect: 'No, not that — try this!' Pair the 'no' tone with a 'yes' alternative.

speech.text: Makes a lot of different sounds like 'mamamama' and 'babababa'

activities.title: Babble Conversation
activities.desc: When they say 'mamama,' respond like they said something meaningful: 'Mama? Yes, I'm here!' This motivates more babbling.

activities.title: New Sounds
activities.desc: Introduce new consonant sounds — 'da da da,' 'na na na,' 'ga ga ga.' Mix them in during play and see if they try!

speech.text: Copies sounds and gestures of others

activities.title: Copy Cat Game
activities.desc: Make a sound or gesture (clapping, waving) and see if they copy. Then copy THEM. It becomes a fun back-and-forth!

activities.title: Action Songs
activities.desc: Sing songs with actions — 'If You're Happy and You Know It,' 'Pat-a-Cake.' They'll start copying the motions!

physical.text: Gets to a sitting position by self

activities.title: Side-Lying Play
activities.desc: Place toys beside them while they're lying down. They'll push up to sit to reach them — building core strength!

activities.title: Roll & Sit
activities.desc: Gently help them practice the rolling-to-sitting sequence. Soon they'll do it on their own!

physical.text: Moves things from one hand to the other

activities.title: Hand Switch
activities.desc: Offer a toy to one hand. Then offer a different toy to the same hand — they'll switch the first to the other hand!

activities.title: Pass the Block
activities.desc: Sit together and pass a block back and forth between hands. Narrate: 'This hand... now THIS hand!'

physical.text: Uses fingers to 'rake' food toward self

activities.title: Cereal Rake
activities.desc: Scatter a few puffs or cheerios on their tray. They'll rake them toward themselves with their fingers to eat!

activities.title: Cracker Crumbs
activities.desc: Break a cracker into small pieces on a plate. Let them practice raking the pieces toward themselves.

physical.text: Sits without support

activities.title: Sitting Play Circle
activities.desc: Place toys in a circle around them while sitting. They'll twist and reach — building balance while playing!

activities.title: Pillow Ring
activities.desc: Place pillows behind and around them (not blocking). They can practice sitting independently with soft backup!

physical.text: Gets into sitting position by themselves

activities.title: Sit Practice
activities.desc: From lying down, see if they can push themselves up to sit. Place toys to the side to motivate twisting and pushing up.

activities.title: Floor Freedom
activities.desc: Give lots of supervised floor time. They need space to practice transitioning between lying, sitting, and crawling.

physical.text: Pulls to stand holding onto furniture

activities.title: Couch Cruising Setup
activities.desc: Place favorite toys on the couch seat. They'll pull up to reach them. Stay close for safety!

activities.title: Standing Play
activities.desc: Hold toys at coffee-table height. They'll pull themselves up on the table edge to play. Celebrate every pull-up!

cognitive.text: Looks for objects when dropped out of sight

activities.title: Drop & Find
activities.desc: Drop a toy off the side of the high chair. Say 'Where did it go?' and lean over to look together!

activities.title: Scarf Pull
activities.desc: Stuff a scarf into a tube (paper towel roll). Let them pull it out — the object was hidden and now it's not!

cognitive.text: Bangs two things together

activities.title: Block Clap
activities.desc: Give them two blocks and show how to bang them together. Clap when they try! The sound is the reward.

activities.title: Pot & Spoon
activities.desc: Give them a wooden spoon and a pot lid. Banging = music-making = cognitive growth. Let them go!

cognitive.text: Explores objects by shaking, banging, and throwing

activities.title: Exploration Station
activities.desc: Offer different objects and let them explore freely — shaking, banging, mouthing, throwing. All are valid!

activities.title: What Sound?
activities.desc: Give them containers with different items inside (rice, bells, fabric). Shake each one — different sounds!

cognitive.text: Pokes at things with pointer finger

activities.title: Button Press
activities.desc: Find safe buttons to poke — toy phones, musical toys, elevator buttons. Pointer finger practice with immediate feedback!

activities.title: Poke the Play-Doh
activities.desc: Make a flat piece of play-doh and let them poke holes in it. Each poke leaves a mark — cause and effect!

cognitive.text: Looks for objects when they see you hide them

activities.title: Blanket Hide
activities.desc: Partially hide a toy under a blanket while they watch. Can they pull the blanket off? That's object permanence!

activities.title: Cup Hide
activities.desc: Hide a small toy under one of two cups. Can they find it? Start easy with the toy sticking out a little.

cognitive.text: Bangs two things together

activities.title: Kitchen Band
activities.desc: Give them two wooden spoons or blocks. Let them bang away! They're learning about cause and effect — and making music!

activities.title: Clap Blocks
activities.desc: Show them how to bang two blocks together, then let them try. Celebrate the noise — it's a cognitive milestone!

social.text: Is shy, clingy, or cautious around new people

activities.title: Safe Introduction
activities.desc: When meeting someone new, hold baby close and let them observe from safety. Don't force interaction — let them warm up.

activities.title: Narrate Newness
activities.desc: Say 'That's Uncle Joe. He's nice!' Stay calm and close. Your calm body language tells them it's safe.

social.text: Shows several facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised)

activities.title: Emotion Mirror
activities.desc: Make faces in the mirror together — happy, sad, surprised, silly. Name each one: 'That's a HAPPY face!'

activities.title: Book Faces
activities.desc: Find books with photos of babies/people showing emotions. Point and name: 'She looks surprised!'

social.text: Lifts arms up to be picked up

activities.title: Arms Up!
activities.desc: Before picking them up, say 'Up! Arms up!' and lift your arms. Wait for them to raise theirs. Then scoop!

activities.title: Model & Wait
activities.desc: Reach your arms down and wait. Don't pick up immediately — give them 3-5 seconds to lift their arms to signal.

social.text: Reacts when you leave (looks, reaches, or cries)

activities.title: Short Separations
activities.desc: Practice brief departures: 'I'll be right back!' Leave for 30 seconds, then return with a big hello. Builds trust.

activities.title: Peekaboo Door
activities.desc: Hide behind a door and peek out: 'Peekaboo!' This teaches object permanence — you always come back.

social.text: Smiles or laughs during peek-a-boo

activities.title: Creative Peekaboo
activities.desc: Use different objects to hide behind — napkin, book, hands, hat. The variety keeps it exciting!

activities.title: Peekaboo Them
activities.desc: Put a light blanket over THEIR head and say 'Where's baby?' They'll pull it off for the reveal — big laughs!

social.text: Has favorite toys and shows preference

activities.title: Choice Offering
activities.desc: Hold out two toys and let them choose. Whichever they reach for is their pick — they're learning to express preferences!

activities.title: Toy Return
activities.desc: If they drop a toy and fuss for it, bring it back. They're learning that they can have preferences and you'll respect them.

speech.text: Waves 'bye-bye'

activities.title: Wave Hello Game
activities.desc: Wave dramatically every time someone enters or leaves a room. Pause and wait for your little one to try waving back!

activities.title: Puppet Waves
activities.desc: Put a sock on your hand like a puppet and make it 'wave.' Let your child wave back. Take turns!

speech.text: Calls parent 'mama' or 'dada' or another special name

activities.title: Name Repeat
activities.desc: Point to yourself and say 'Mama!' or 'Dada!' slowly and clearly. Pause and smile — let them try to copy the sound.

activities.title: Photo Game
activities.desc: Show photos of family members on your phone. Point and name each person: 'That's Mama!' Repeat throughout the day.

speech.text: Understands 'no' (pauses or stops briefly)

activities.title: Gentle No Game
activities.desc: When they reach for something off-limits, say 'No, no' gently with a head shake. Then redirect to a safe item and say 'Yes! This one!'

activities.title: Stop & Go
activities.desc: Play music and dance together. When you stop the music, say 'Stop!' with a hand up. When it starts, say 'Go!' and dance again.

speech.text: Points to show you something

activities.title: Window Watching
activities.desc: Sit by a window together. Point to things outside — 'Look! A bird!' Wait for them to point at something too.

activities.title: Point & Name Walk
activities.desc: During a walk or around the house, point to objects and name them. Encourage your child to point at things that interest them.

speech.text: Tries to say one or two words besides 'mama' or 'dada' (like 'ba' for ball)

activities.title: First Words Hunt
activities.desc: Listen for any consistent sound they use for an object — 'ba' for ball, 'na' for snack. That counts as a word!

activities.title: Label Everything
activities.desc: Narrate your day: 'Here's your CUP. Let's get your SHOES.' Emphasize key words — they're soaking it all in.

speech.text: Responds to simple spoken requests (like 'give me the ball')

activities.title: Simple Requests
activities.desc: Try 'Give me the cup' while holding out your hand. If they respond, celebrate big! Use gestures to help.

activities.title: Two-Step Play
activities.desc: During play, try simple directions: 'Get the ball' and 'Bring it here.' Keep it fun and celebrate any attempt!

physical.text: Pulls up to stand

activities.title: Couch Cruise
activities.desc: Place a favorite toy just out of reach on the couch. Encourage them to pull up on the cushion to grab it. Celebrate when they do!

activities.title: Laundry Basket Stand
activities.desc: Turn a sturdy laundry basket upside down. Put toys on top and let them pull up to play with them.

physical.text: Walks holding on to furniture

activities.title: Furniture Trail
activities.desc: Line up furniture pieces close together to create a 'path.' Place toys along the way to motivate them to cruise from piece to piece.

activities.title: Dance Cruise
activities.desc: Play music and hold their hands while they stand. Gently sway. Then let them hold the furniture edge while you dance nearby!

physical.text: Drinks from a cup without a lid, with help

activities.title: Sip & Celebrate
activities.desc: At mealtimes, offer a small open cup with just a little water. Help them tip it and cheer each sip!

activities.title: Tea Party
activities.desc: Have a pretend tea party with small cups. Practice 'sipping' together — even empty cups help build the motion!

physical.text: Picks things up between thumb and pointer finger

activities.title: Cheerio Pick-Up
activities.desc: Scatter a few cereal pieces on their tray. Let them practice picking up one at a time with their fingers to eat.

activities.title: Sticker Peel
activities.desc: Partially peel stickers off a sheet. Let them grip the edge and pull the sticker off. Stick them on paper together!

physical.text: Walks while holding onto furniture (cruising)

activities.title: Furniture Path
activities.desc: Arrange furniture so they can cruise from one piece to the next. Place toys along the path for motivation!

activities.title: Hand-Hold Walking
activities.desc: Offer your fingers for them to hold while walking. Let them lead — they'll let go when they're ready!

physical.text: Uses a pincer grasp (thumb and pointer finger) to pick up small things

activities.title: Cheerio Challenge
activities.desc: Place small, safe foods (puffs, Cheerios) on their tray. Picking them up with two fingers is a major fine motor skill!

activities.title: Small Object Play
activities.desc: Offer large beads, big buttons, or chunky crayons to practice picking up with thumb and finger.

cognitive.text: Puts things in a container

activities.title: Drop & Dump
activities.desc: Give them a bucket and soft blocks. Show them how to drop blocks in, then dump them out. Repeat — they love it!

activities.title: Sock Basket
activities.desc: Roll up socks into balls. Hold a basket and let them toss the socks in. Count each one!

cognitive.text: Looks for things they see you hide

activities.title: Blanket Hide
activities.desc: While they watch, hide a toy under a blanket. Say 'Where did it go?' and let them pull the blanket to find it!

activities.title: Cup Shuffle
activities.desc: Put a small toy under one of two cups while they watch. Ask 'Where is it?' and let them lift a cup to find it.

cognitive.text: Explores things in different ways (shaking, throwing)

activities.title: Kitchen Band
activities.desc: Give them a wooden spoon and some pots/containers. Let them bang, shake, and explore the different sounds!

activities.title: Texture Tour
activities.desc: Gather items with different textures (smooth cup, fuzzy sock, crinkly paper). Let them feel, shake, and explore each one.

cognitive.text: Follows simple directions like 'pick up the toy'

activities.title: Clean-Up Song
activities.desc: Sing 'Clean up, clean up!' and hand them a toy to put in a bin. Celebrate when they follow through!

activities.title: Fetch Game
activities.desc: Roll a ball and say 'Go get it!' When they bring it back, cheer and roll it again. Simple directions + fun!

cognitive.text: Puts things in a container and takes them out

activities.title: In-Out Box
activities.desc: Give them a container and small toys. Show them 'in!' and 'out!' They'll love dumping and refilling.

activities.title: Drop Zone
activities.desc: Cut a hole in a shoebox lid. Show them how to drop balls in and open the box. Cause and effect magic!

cognitive.text: Pokes with index (pointer) finger

activities.title: Button Push
activities.desc: Show them toys with buttons — musical toys, light-up buttons. They'll poke with their pointer finger to make things happen!

activities.title: Poking Play
activities.desc: Poke at playdough, bubbles, or soft food together. Say 'poke poke!' They love the cause and effect.

social.text: Plays games with you like pat-a-cake

activities.title: Pat-a-Cake Party
activities.desc: Sing pat-a-cake while clapping their hands together with yours. Add their name to the song to make it personal!

activities.title: Peek-a-Boo Variations
activities.desc: Play peek-a-boo with different objects — behind a pillow, under a hat, around a corner. Each variation builds anticipation!

social.text: Has favorite things and people

activities.title: Photo Favorites
activities.desc: Show pictures of family members and name them. Watch which photos make your child smile or reach — those are favorites!

activities.title: Lovey Show & Tell
activities.desc: Gather their favorite toys. Hold each one up and say its name. Let them reach for the one they want most!

social.text: Shows fear in some situations

activities.title: Brave Together
activities.desc: When they seem scared of something new, stay calm and narrate: 'That's a big dog! Let's watch from here.' Your calm teaches calm.

activities.title: Comfort Corner
activities.desc: Create a cozy spot with pillows and a favorite toy. When things feel big, bring them there to feel safe and recover.

social.text: Repeats sounds or actions to get attention

activities.title: Copy Cat
activities.desc: When they make a sound, copy it back! Then add a new sound and wait for them to imitate you. Take turns!

activities.title: Sound Mirror
activities.desc: Sit face-to-face. Make a funny sound or face. When they copy you, cheer! Then let them lead and you copy them.

social.text: Plays games with you, like pat-a-cake or peek-a-boo

activities.title: Pat-a-Cake Pro
activities.desc: Sing pat-a-cake and do the clapping motions. They'll start learning the pattern and joining in!

activities.title: Peek-a-Boo Variations
activities.desc: Try different versions — hide behind a blanket, around a corner, under a hat. Each variation is a new game!

social.text: Cries when a parent leaves (separation awareness)

activities.title: Bye-Bye Practice
activities.desc: Always say goodbye before leaving (don't sneak out). Short separations with a calm goodbye build trust.

activities.title: Return Ritual
activities.desc: When you come back, give a big warm hello. They learn that you always come back — this reduces anxiety over time.

speech.text: Tries to say 1-2 words besides 'mama' or 'dada'

activities.title: Word of the Day
activities.desc: Pick one word to emphasize all day: 'Ball! Look, a ball! Throw the ball!' Repetition is key.

activities.title: First Words Book
activities.desc: Use a simple picture book and point: 'Dog! Dog!' Say the word clearly and slowly. Wait for them to try.

speech.text: Looks at a familiar object when you name it

activities.title: Where Is It?
activities.desc: Ask 'Where's your cup?' or 'Where's the ball?' Wait for them to look toward it. Big celebration when they do!

activities.title: Room Tour
activities.desc: Walk around the room pointing and naming: 'Light! Door! Chair!' They'll start looking at objects when you name them.

speech.text: Follows directions given with both a gesture and words

activities.title: Show & Tell Directions
activities.desc: Point at the ball AND say 'Give me the ball.' The gesture + words combo helps them understand.

activities.title: Action Songs
activities.desc: 'If You're Happy and You Know It' — clap your hands (gesture) while singing (words). They follow both!

speech.text: Points to ask for something or to get help

activities.title: Wait & Point
activities.desc: When they want something, wait before giving it. Model pointing: 'Point to what you want!' Then give it.

activities.title: Snack Choices
activities.desc: Hold up two snacks and wait. When they reach, guide their hand to point. 'You're pointing to the banana!'

speech.text: Tries to say 3 or more words besides 'mama' and 'dada'

activities.title: Word Catching
activities.desc: Keep a mental list of words they attempt. 'Ba' for ball, 'mo' for more — any consistent sound for an object counts!

activities.title: Choice Words
activities.desc: Offer two things and name each one: 'Banana or apple?' When they respond with any sound, repeat the word clearly.

speech.text: Looks at a familiar object when you name it

activities.title: Where's the...?
activities.desc: Ask 'Where's the doggy?' or 'Where's your cup?' See if they look at or point to the right thing!

activities.title: Book Pointing
activities.desc: Read a book and ask 'Where's the cat?' See if they point to it. This shows they understand the word-object connection.

physical.text: Takes a few steps on their own

activities.title: Step to Me
activities.desc: Kneel a few steps away with arms out. Say 'Walk to me!' Catch them with a hug when they arrive.

activities.title: Push Toy Walk
activities.desc: Give them a push toy (laundry basket, toy cart) for stability. It gives confidence for independent steps!

physical.text: Uses fingers to feed themselves

activities.title: Finger Food Buffet
activities.desc: Offer a variety of small, soft finger foods. Let them pick and choose — it's fine motor AND independence!

activities.title: Dip & Eat
activities.desc: Give them soft food with a dip (hummus, yogurt). They'll pinch food and dip it — advanced finger work!

physical.text: Stands alone without holding on

activities.title: Hands-Free Play
activities.desc: While they're standing and holding furniture, offer a toy that needs two hands. They'll let go to grab it!

activities.title: Bubble Pop Stand
activities.desc: Blow bubbles while they're standing. They'll let go of furniture to reach up and pop them!

physical.text: May walk up stairs with help

activities.title: Stair Climb
activities.desc: Hold both hands and practice going up 2-3 steps. Count each step together. Always supervise closely!

activities.title: Step Up Play
activities.desc: Find a low, stable step (or use a sturdy box). Practice stepping up and down with your support.

physical.text: Takes a few steps on their own

activities.title: Step to Me
activities.desc: Sit a few feet away and hold out your arms: 'Walk to me!' Even 2-3 wobbly steps is amazing. Celebrate every one!

activities.title: Push Toy Walking
activities.desc: Offer a push toy or small shopping cart. Walking behind something sturdy builds confidence for solo steps.

physical.text: Uses fingers to feed self some food

activities.title: Self-Feeding Fun
activities.desc: Put small bite-sized foods on the tray and let them pick up and eat independently. It's messy but important!

activities.title: Dip & Eat
activities.desc: Give them soft foods they can dip a finger into — hummus, yogurt. Bringing finger to mouth is fine motor + self-care.

cognitive.text: Tries to use things the right way (phone, cup, book)

activities.title: Pretend Play
activities.desc: Give them a play phone and hold one too. Say 'Hello? Hi!' and hold it to your ear. They'll copy!

activities.title: Book Right Side Up
activities.desc: Hand them a book upside down. Watch — they may flip it the right way! They're learning how objects work.

cognitive.text: Stacks at least 2 small objects

activities.title: Stack & Cheer
activities.desc: Stack 2 blocks and let them add one more. Cheer! Then let them knock it down and start over — that's learning too!

activities.title: Cup Tower
activities.desc: Use plastic cups — easier to stack than blocks! Start with 2 and work up. Celebrate every stack!

cognitive.text: Explores how objects work (pushes buttons, turns knobs)

activities.title: Knob & Button Tour
activities.desc: Find safe knobs and buttons around the house. Let them explore: light switches, radio knobs, toy buttons.

activities.title: Busy Board
activities.desc: Create a simple busy board: attach a latch, a light switch, and a zipper to a board. Endless exploration!

cognitive.text: Points at what they want

activities.title: Point Power
activities.desc: When they point at something, immediately name it: 'You want the cup!' Responsive naming builds language.

activities.title: Choice Board
activities.desc: Hold up 2-3 items and let them point to choose. 'What do you want? Point!' Respects their communication.

cognitive.text: Stacks at least 2 small objects, like blocks

activities.title: Block Tower
activities.desc: Stack 2 blocks and say 'Look, a tower!' Then knock it down (they love that part). Let them try stacking!

activities.title: Cup Stack
activities.desc: Use stacking cups or small containers. Show them how to stack 2 and celebrate when they try!

cognitive.text: Copies you doing chores, like sweeping with a broom

activities.title: Mini Helper
activities.desc: Give them a small broom, dustpan, or cloth. They'll imitate what you do — that's advanced cognitive development!

activities.title: Copy Cooking
activities.desc: Give them a bowl and spoon while you cook. They'll stir and pretend right alongside you!

social.text: Copies other children while playing

activities.title: Parallel Play
activities.desc: Set up play dates or park visits. They'll watch other kids and start copying. No interaction needed yet — just watching!

activities.title: Sibling Copy
activities.desc: Have an older child or sibling do simple actions (clapping, dancing). Toddlers LOVE copying older kids!

social.text: Shows you an object they like

activities.title: Show Me!
activities.desc: When they bring you a toy, respond enthusiastically: 'Wow, you found a block! Show me!' This reinforces sharing joy.

activities.title: Discovery Reaction
activities.desc: React big when they hold something up to you: 'What did you find?!' Your excitement motivates more showing.

social.text: Claps when excited

activities.title: Clap Along
activities.desc: Clap after every small success: finished eating, built a tower, put on a shoe. They'll start clapping too!

activities.title: Clapping Songs
activities.desc: Sing songs with clapping: 'If You're Happy and You Know It' — the clapping part is the best!

social.text: Hugs a stuffed doll or toy

activities.title: Teddy Hug
activities.desc: Hold a stuffed animal and hug it: 'Aww, hug the teddy!' Give it to them. Model the gentle hug.

activities.title: Goodnight Hugs
activities.desc: At bedtime, have them hug each stuffed animal goodnight. 'Hug the bear! Hug the bunny!' Sweet routine.

social.text: Shows you affection (hugs, cuddles, or kisses)

activities.title: Affection Time
activities.desc: Throughout the day, ask for hugs and kisses: 'Can I have a hug?' Always respond warmly when they give one!

activities.title: Love Circle
activities.desc: Include affection in routines: goodbye hug, hello kiss, bedtime cuddle. Predictable affection builds security.

social.text: Shows you an object they like by holding it out

activities.title: Show & Tell
activities.desc: When they hold something out to show you, react with enthusiasm: 'Wow, a ball! Thank you for showing me!' This reinforces sharing.

activities.title: Trade Game
activities.desc: Show them something interesting and hold it out. When they take it and show you something back, you're having a conversation!

speech.text: Tries to say 3 or more words besides 'mama' or 'dada'

activities.title: Narrate Everything
activities.desc: As you go through daily routines, name everything: 'Shoe! Banana! Cup!' Keep it simple and repeat often.

activities.title: Animal Sounds
activities.desc: Look at animal pictures and make the sounds together. 'The cow says... moo!' Animal sounds count as words!

speech.text: Follows one-step directions without gestures

activities.title: Helper Time
activities.desc: Give one simple direction at a time: 'Bring me the spoon' or 'Put it on the table.' Cheer when they do it!

activities.title: Toy Delivery
activities.desc: Ask them to 'Give the ball to Daddy' or 'Put the bear on the chair.' Make it a fun delivery game!

speech.text: Points to show you something interesting

activities.title: Discovery Walk
activities.desc: On a walk, stop and point at interesting things — a flower, a truck, a dog. Say 'Look!' and wait for them to point too.

activities.title: Picture Hunt
activities.desc: Open a colorful book. Point to pictures and name them. Ask 'Where's the cat?' and let them point!

speech.text: Says 'no' and shakes head

activities.title: Yes/No Game
activities.desc: Hold up two items — one they love, one they don't. 'Do you want this?' Practice yes (nod) and no (shake).

activities.title: Silly Offers
activities.desc: Offer silly things: 'Do you want to eat a shoe?' They'll say 'No!' and laugh. Mix in real offers too.

speech.text: Says words to ask for something (like 'more' or 'milk')

activities.title: Pause & Wait
activities.desc: Instead of anticipating their every need, pause and wait for them to use a word. Even an attempt counts!

activities.title: Model Words
activities.desc: Model the word you want them to use: 'You want MORE? More milk? More!' Repetition is key.

speech.text: Points to show you something interesting

activities.title: Point & Discover
activities.desc: When they point at something, name it with excitement: 'You see the airplane! Wow!' This builds vocabulary fast.

activities.title: Walk & Point
activities.desc: Go on a walk and point out things together. 'Look, a dog!' Soon they'll be pointing at everything!

physical.text: Walks without holding on to anyone or anything

activities.title: Walk to Me
activities.desc: Sit a few feet away and hold out your arms. Say 'Walk to me!' and cheer every step. Gradually increase distance.

activities.title: Bubble Chase
activities.desc: Blow bubbles and let them walk (or toddle!) to pop them. Bubbles are great motivation to move!

physical.text: Scribbles

activities.title: Big Paper Scribble
activities.desc: Tape a large piece of paper to the table or floor. Give them a fat crayon and let them go wild!

activities.title: Water Drawing
activities.desc: Give them a paintbrush and a cup of water. Let them 'paint' on the sidewalk or a dark surface — scribbles that disappear!

physical.text: Drinks from a cup without a lid (may spill)

activities.title: Tiny Sips
activities.desc: Use a small cup with just a little water. Practice at every meal — spills are okay! They're learning.

activities.title: Pour Practice
activities.desc: During bath time, give them small cups to pour water back and forth. Builds the same muscle control!

physical.text: Feeds self with fingers

activities.title: Snack Tray
activities.desc: Put a few different finger foods on a plate (cut small!). Let them pick and choose what to eat. Independence!

activities.title: Picnic Play
activities.desc: Have a mini picnic on the floor with finger foods on a blanket. The novelty encourages self-feeding!

physical.text: Tries to use a spoon

activities.title: Scoop & Dump
activities.desc: Give them a spoon and a bowl of dry cereal or puffs. Let them practice scooping — even if most falls off!

activities.title: Yogurt Art
activities.desc: Put yogurt on a plate and give them a spoon. Let them scoop, smear, and eventually taste. It's messy but great practice!

physical.text: Eats with a spoon (messy but trying)

activities.title: Spoon Loading
activities.desc: Load the spoon for them and let them bring it to their mouth. Thick foods (yogurt, oatmeal) are easier to start with!

activities.title: Practice Scooping
activities.desc: Let them practice scooping rice, sand, or water with a spoon outside of mealtime. Less pressure, more fun!

cognitive.text: Copies you doing chores (sweeping, wiping)

activities.title: Mini Helper
activities.desc: Give them a small broom, cloth, or sponge while you clean. Narrate: 'We're wiping the table!' They love feeling included.

activities.title: Copy Cat Chores
activities.desc: Dramatically do a chore slowly — wiping, sweeping, folding. Pause and hand them the tool. Watch them imitate!

cognitive.text: Plays with toys in a simple way (pushing a car)

activities.title: Car Ramp
activities.desc: Prop a cookie sheet or board on a pillow to make a ramp. Show them how to push a car down. Vroom!
activities.materials: Toy car + flat surface for ramp

activities.title: Ball Roll
activities.desc: Sit across from each other and roll a ball back and forth. Add sound effects: 'Here it comes!' Simple, purposeful play.

cognitive.text: Can identify one or more body parts when asked

activities.title: Head, Shoulders Song
activities.desc: Sing 'Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes' slowly, touching each part. Let them follow along at their own pace.

activities.title: Where's Your Nose?
activities.desc: Ask 'Where's your nose?' and point to yours. When they point to theirs, cheer! Work through: eyes, ears, mouth, tummy.

cognitive.text: Stacks blocks or objects on top of each other

activities.title: Stack & Crash
activities.desc: Stack blocks (or food containers) together. Let them knock the tower down — then build again! Crash is the best part.

activities.title: Food Cup Tower
activities.desc: Use plastic cups to build a tower at the table. Stack 2-3 and let them add one on top (or knock it over!).

cognitive.text: Tries to use everyday objects correctly (phone, cup, book)

activities.title: Pretend Call
activities.desc: Give them a play phone and say 'Call grandma!' Watch if they hold it to their ear — that's understanding function!

activities.title: Object Use Show
activities.desc: Show them how everyday items work — brush hair with a brush, drink from a cup. Then let them try!

cognitive.text: Can follow simple directions without gestures (like 'get your shoes')

activities.title: Hide & Find
activities.desc: Ask them to go get a specific item without pointing: 'Get your shoes.' If they do it, they understand the words!

activities.title: Helper Tasks
activities.desc: Give simple one-step tasks: 'Put this in the trash' or 'Bring me the book.' Celebrate every success!

social.text: Moves away from you but looks to make sure you're close

activities.title: Base Camp
activities.desc: Let them explore a safe space while you sit nearby. When they look back, smile and wave — showing you're their safe base.

activities.title: Nearby Narration
activities.desc: While they play independently, narrate from a distance: 'You found a block!' This keeps connection without hovering.

social.text: Puts hands out for you to wash them

activities.title: Wash Time Song
activities.desc: Sing a short song every time you wash hands. Soon they'll put their hands out when they hear it!

activities.title: Routine Cue
activities.desc: Before meals, say 'Time to wash!' and hold out your hands. Wait for them to copy. Make it a predictable routine.

social.text: Looks at a few pages in a book with you

activities.title: Point & Chat
activities.desc: Don't worry about reading every word. Just point at pictures and chat: 'Look, a bunny! What's he doing?' Let them turn pages.

activities.title: Touch & Feel Books
activities.desc: Books with textures keep little hands busy. Let them feel each page while you name what they're touching.

social.text: Helps dress self (pushes arm through sleeve)

activities.title: Arm Push Game
activities.desc: Hold the sleeve open wide and say 'Push!' as they push their arm through. Make it a celebration each time!

activities.title: Hat On/Off
activities.desc: Put a hat on and take it off — then let them try. Hats, socks, and shoes are great 'dressing practice' starters.

social.text: Climbs on and off a couch or chair without help

activities.title: Pillow Mountain
activities.desc: Make a small pillow pile near the couch. Let them climb up and over safely. Supervise closely — they love the challenge!

activities.title: Step Up Practice
activities.desc: Find a low, stable surface (like an ottoman). Show them how to climb up and sit, then slide off feet-first.

social.text: May be afraid of strangers or new situations

activities.title: Safe Base
activities.desc: In new situations, let them stay close to you and observe. Don't force interaction — being cautious is healthy development!

activities.title: Gradual Exposure
activities.desc: Introduce new people and places slowly. Start with short visits and build up. Your calm presence is their security.

speech.text: Points to things in a book when asked

activities.title: Find the Picture
activities.desc: Open a picture book and ask 'Where's the dog?' Wait for them to point. Celebrate and name it!

activities.title: Magazine Hunt
activities.desc: Flip through a magazine together. Ask them to find things: 'Show me a baby!' or 'Where's the car?'

speech.text: Says at least 2 words together like 'more milk'

activities.title: Expansion Game
activities.desc: When they say one word ('milk'), add to it: 'More milk? Yes, more milk!' Model the 2-word phrase naturally.

activities.title: Choice Time
activities.desc: Hold up two items and model: 'Want milk? Want juice?' They'll start combining: 'want milk!' Give what they ask for!

speech.text: Points to at least 2 body parts when asked

activities.title: Body Part Song
activities.desc: Sing 'Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes' — go slowly and touch each part. They'll start pointing along!

activities.title: Sticker Body
activities.desc: Put stickers on different body parts and say 'Where's the sticker? On your nose!' Let them find and peel them.

speech.text: Uses more gestures than just waving (blows a kiss, nods yes)

activities.title: Gesture Songs
activities.desc: Sing 'Itsy Bitsy Spider' or 'Wheels on the Bus' with big gestures. Pause and let them fill in the motions!

activities.title: Blow Kiss Goodnight
activities.desc: Make blowing kisses part of bedtime or goodbye routine. They'll learn to do it back. Add thumbs up, high-fives too!

speech.text: Says at least 50 words

activities.title: Word Diary
activities.desc: Keep a running list of all their words. You might be surprised — animal sounds, names, and attempts all count!

activities.title: New Word of the Day
activities.desc: Pick a fun new word each day and use it often. 'Today's word is ELEPHANT! Look at the elephant!' Repeat, repeat!

speech.text: Puts two words together (like 'more milk' or 'daddy go')

activities.title: Expand & Model
activities.desc: When they say one word, add another: 'Milk?' → 'More milk!' Model the two-word phrase without correcting them.

activities.title: Action + Object
activities.desc: During play, model two-word combos: 'Throw ball,' 'eat banana,' 'push car.' Keep it simple and repeat often.

physical.text: Kicks a ball

activities.title: Ball Kickaround
activities.desc: Roll a soft ball to their feet and say 'Kick it!' Start close and move further back as they get the hang of it.

activities.title: Target Practice
activities.desc: Set up a laundry basket on its side. Show them how to kick the ball into the 'goal.' Cheer every attempt!

physical.text: Runs

activities.title: Chase Game
activities.desc: Say 'I'm gonna get you!' and chase them (slowly!). Then let them chase you. Running with a purpose!

activities.title: Race to the Tree
activities.desc: Pick a landmark (tree, fence, door). Say 'Ready, set, GO!' and run together. Let them win sometimes!

physical.text: Walks up a few stairs with or without help

activities.title: One Step at a Time
activities.desc: Hold their hand and practice stairs. Count each step: 'One... two... three!' Celebrate at the top.

activities.title: Teddy Goes Upstairs
activities.desc: Have them carry a stuffed animal up the stairs: 'Let's help teddy go up!' Adds motivation and purpose.

physical.text: Eats with a spoon

activities.title: Scoop Challenge
activities.desc: Serve thick foods (oatmeal, yogurt, mashed potatoes) that stick to the spoon. Easier to scoop = more success!

activities.title: Practice Bowl
activities.desc: Put dry cereal in a bowl and let them practice scooping with a spoon. No mess, all the practice!

physical.text: Kicks a ball

activities.title: Ball Kick
activities.desc: Place a ball in front of them and show them how to kick. Start with a big, lightweight ball that's easy to connect with!

activities.title: Target Kick
activities.desc: Set up two cones or shoes as a 'goal.' Stand behind and cheer when they kick toward it!

physical.text: Runs short distances

activities.title: Chase Me!
activities.desc: Play a gentle game of chase. Let them run ahead and 'catch' them with a hug. Running builds balance and coordination.

activities.title: Race to the...
activities.desc: 'Let's run to the tree!' Pick short distances and race together. Let them win sometimes!

cognitive.text: Holds something in one hand while using the other

activities.title: Stamp Art
activities.desc: Hold paper with one hand, stamp with the other. Use sponges or cookie cutters dipped in paint!

activities.title: Banana Peel
activities.desc: Let them hold a banana in one hand while you start the peel. Then let them pull it with the other hand!

cognitive.text: Tries to use switches, knobs, or buttons on a toy

activities.title: Light Switch Fun
activities.desc: Hold them up to a light switch. Say 'On!' (flip up) 'Off!' (flip down). They'll love the cause and effect!

activities.title: Button Hunt
activities.desc: Find safe buttons around the house: elevator, microwave timer, doorbell. Let them push and see what happens!

cognitive.text: Plays with more than one toy at the same time

activities.title: Teddy Tea Party
activities.desc: Set up a pretend tea party with a stuffed animal and play cups. They'll use multiple toys together in one scene!

activities.title: Car & Block City
activities.desc: Build a simple road with blocks, then drive toy cars on it. Combining toys = growing imagination!

cognitive.text: Begins to sort shapes and colors

activities.title: Sock Match
activities.desc: Dump a pile of different-colored socks. Help them match pairs by color: 'Find another blue one!'

activities.title: Snack Sort
activities.desc: Put different colored snacks on a plate (berries, crackers, grapes). Sort them by color into groups before eating!

cognitive.text: Plays with more than one toy at the same time (puts toy food on a toy plate)

activities.title: Tea Party
activities.desc: Set up a pretend tea party. Show them how to put food on plates and pour 'tea.' Combining toys shows advanced thinking!

activities.title: Build & Play
activities.desc: Build a road with blocks and drive cars on it. Using toys together shows cognitive connections!

cognitive.text: Shows simple problem-solving (like standing on a stool to reach something)

activities.title: Puzzle Challenge
activities.desc: Offer simple 3-4 piece puzzles. Let them struggle a little before helping — problem-solving builds resilience!

activities.title: Reaching Games
activities.desc: Place a desired toy slightly out of reach (safely). Watch if they problem-solve — use a stool, ask for help, or find a tool.

social.text: Notices when others are hurt or upset

activities.title: Feelings Faces
activities.desc: Make different faces (happy, sad, surprised) and name them. Ask 'How does teddy feel?' when playing.

activities.title: Band-Aid Game
activities.desc: When a toy 'falls down,' say 'Oh no, teddy has a boo-boo!' Give them a pretend band-aid to 'help' the toy.

social.text: Looks at your face to see how to react in a new situation

activities.title: Model It
activities.desc: When something surprising happens, narrate your reaction: 'Whoa, that was loud! But we're okay!' They learn from watching you.

activities.title: New Food Face
activities.desc: Try a new food together. Make a big 'Mmm!' face. They'll look at you first, then often try it too!

social.text: Shows defiant behavior (doing what they've been told not to)

activities.title: Choices Power
activities.desc: Instead of 'Put on shoes,' try 'Red shoes or blue shoes?' Giving choices reduces power struggles.

activities.title: Yes Jar
activities.desc: Find more things to say 'yes' to each day. 'Yes, you CAN help stir!' saves 'no' for the important stuff.

social.text: Gets excited when with other children

activities.title: Parallel Play Date
activities.desc: Play alongside another child with similar toys. They don't need to share yet — just being near each other builds social skills!

activities.title: Park Narration
activities.desc: At a playground, narrate: 'Look at that kid going down the slide! Want to try?' Help bridge the gap to interaction.

social.text: Looks at your face to see how to react in a new situation

activities.title: Calm Model
activities.desc: In new situations, show calm confidence. They'll look at your face to decide if something is scary or safe.

activities.title: Narrate Reactions
activities.desc: When something surprising happens, narrate: 'Oh! That was loud but it's okay!' They learn emotional regulation from you.

social.text: Plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them

activities.title: Parallel Play
activities.desc: Set up play dates where kids play side by side. Don't force sharing — playing NEAR each other is the developmental stage!

activities.title: Turn Taking
activities.desc: Practice simple turn-taking: 'Your turn to stack, now my turn!' This is the foundation of playing WITH others.

speech.text: Talks with you in conversation (at least 2 back-and-forth exchanges)

activities.title: Tell Me About It
activities.desc: Ask open questions: 'What did you do today?' or 'What happened next?' Keep the conversation going back and forth!

activities.title: Phone Call
activities.desc: Pretend to call them on a toy phone. Have a mini conversation: 'Hello! What are you doing? Oh wow!' They practice chatting.

speech.text: Asks 'who,' 'what,' 'where,' or 'why' questions

activities.title: Question Box
activities.desc: Put mystery objects in a box. Pull one out and model: 'What is this? Where does it go?' Encourage their questions too!

activities.title: Curiosity Walk
activities.desc: On a walk, wonder out loud: 'Why is the sky blue? Where do birds sleep?' Let them ask 'why' — it's a sign of growth!

speech.text: Says what action is happening in a picture (running, eating)

activities.title: Action Book
activities.desc: Look at picture books and ask 'What is she doing?' Model if needed: 'She's running!' Then let them try the next one.

activities.title: Act It Out
activities.desc: Take turns acting out actions (jumping, sleeping, eating). The other person guesses: 'You're jumping!'

speech.text: Says first name

activities.title: Name Song
activities.desc: Make up a simple song with their name: 'Emma, Emma, that's my name!' Sing it at bath time or in the car.

activities.title: Roll Call
activities.desc: Play 'school' — call out names of family members, stuffed animals, and them! When you say their name, they say 'Here!'

speech.text: Talks well enough for others to understand most of the time

activities.title: Slow Down & Repeat
activities.desc: When you don't understand, say 'Tell me again?' calmly. Then repeat back what they said correctly: 'Oh, you want the TRUCK!'

activities.title: Story Time Narrator
activities.desc: Let them 'read' a familiar book to you using their own words. Don't correct — just listen and respond!

speech.text: Talks well enough for most people outside the family to understand

activities.title: Restaurant Order
activities.desc: Let them order their own food at a restaurant. It's great practice speaking to unfamiliar adults!

activities.title: Story Retell
activities.desc: After reading a story, ask them to tell it back to you. Listen for clear pronunciation and gently model any tricky words.

physical.text: Strings items together (beads, macaroni)

activities.title: Pasta Necklace
activities.desc: String large pasta (penne, rigatoni) onto a shoelace or yarn. Paint them first for extra fun!

activities.title: Cheerio Bracelet
activities.desc: String Cheerios onto a pipe cleaner to make a bracelet. They can eat it afterward as a snack!

physical.text: Puts on some clothes by self

activities.title: Dress-Up Race
activities.desc: Put out easy clothes (big shirts, hats, scarves) and have a silly dress-up race. Speed isn't the point — independence is!

activities.title: Backwards Day
activities.desc: Let them pick their own outfit — even if it's mismatched or backwards. Practicing the skill matters more than perfection!

physical.text: Uses a fork

activities.title: Fork Fun Foods
activities.desc: Serve fork-friendly foods: soft cubes of fruit, pasta, or cheese. Show them how to stab and lift.

activities.title: Play-Doh Dinner
activities.desc: Make Play-Doh food and practice stabbing it with a fork. Great practice without the mess of real food!

physical.text: Pedals a tricycle

activities.title: Feet on Pedals
activities.desc: Start by placing their feet on the pedals and pushing them gently. Then let them try pushing on their own.

activities.title: Destination Ride
activities.desc: Set a 'finish line' (a cone, a tree). Say 'Pedal to the finish!' Having a goal motivates the effort.

physical.text: Strings large beads or items onto a string

activities.title: Bead Threading
activities.desc: Use large wooden beads and a thick shoelace. Threading builds fine motor skills, hand-eye coordination, and patience!

activities.title: Pasta Necklace
activities.desc: Thread large pasta (penne, rigatoni) onto yarn. They can paint them later for a craft-and-skills combo!

physical.text: Pedals a tricycle

activities.title: Pedal Practice
activities.desc: Show them the push-push motion on the pedals. Start on flat ground and let them go short distances.

activities.title: Path Riding
activities.desc: Draw a chalk path on the sidewalk and have them ride along it. This adds a fun challenge to pedaling!

cognitive.text: Draws a circle when you show them how

activities.title: Circle Hunt
activities.desc: Find circles around the house (plates, clocks, buttons). Then try drawing one together on paper!

activities.title: Finger Paint Circle
activities.desc: Use pudding or shaving cream on a tray. Draw a circle with your finger and let them trace over it.

cognitive.text: Avoids touching hot objects when warned

activities.title: Hot/Cold Game
activities.desc: Practice with warm (not hot) and cold items: 'This is warm! We don't touch hot things.' Naming builds understanding.

activities.title: Kitchen Safety Talk
activities.desc: While cooking, point: 'The stove is hot — we stay back!' Repeat consistently. They learn through repetition.

cognitive.text: Does puzzles with 3-4 pieces

activities.title: Homemade Puzzle
activities.desc: Cut a photo or drawing into 3-4 pieces. Let them put it back together. Start with 2 pieces and add more!

activities.title: Shape Match
activities.desc: Cut shapes out of cardboard and trace their outline on paper. They match each shape to its outline!

cognitive.text: Turns pages of a book one at a time

activities.title: Page Turner
activities.desc: During story time, let them turn each page. Say 'Can you turn the page?' and wait. Board books are easier to start.

activities.title: Sticky Note Books
activities.desc: Put one sticky note on each page of a book. They peel off the note AND turn the page — double practice!

cognitive.text: Draws a circle when you show them how

activities.title: Circle Drawing
activities.desc: Draw a big circle and say 'round and round!' Let them try. Any closed roundish shape counts!

activities.title: Trace & Draw
