activities.desc: Draw circles on a page and let them trace over yours first, then try their own. Celebrate every attempt!

cognitive.text: Turns pages in a book one at a time

activities.title: Page Turner
activities.desc: Let them be in charge of turning pages during reading time. Board books are easiest; paper books are the next challenge.

activities.title: Find the Picture
activities.desc: Ask them to turn to find a specific picture: 'Where's the cat? Keep turning!' This practices page-turning with purpose.

social.text: Calms down within 10 minutes after you leave

activities.title: Goodbye Routine
activities.desc: Create a quick, predictable goodbye: hug, wave, 'See you soon!' Keeping it short and cheerful builds confidence.

activities.title: Comfort Object
activities.desc: Let them bring a favorite toy or blanket when separating. Having something familiar helps them self-soothe.

social.text: Notices other children and joins them to play

activities.title: Playground Coach
activities.desc: At the park, narrate: 'That boy is playing with blocks. Want to play next to him?' Help bridge the gap gently.

activities.title: Playdate Practice
activities.desc: Start with one-on-one playdates. Smaller groups are less overwhelming. Guide simple interactions: 'You could show him your truck!'

social.text: Shows concern for a crying friend

activities.title: What Would Teddy Do?
activities.desc: During play, if teddy 'cries,' ask: 'Oh no, teddy's sad. What should we do?' They practice empathy through pretend.

activities.title: Feelings Check-In
activities.desc: When you see someone sad (in real life or a book), say: 'She looks sad. How could we help?' Name the feeling first.

social.text: Takes turns in games

activities.title: My Turn, Your Turn
activities.desc: Play a simple game (stacking blocks, rolling a ball). Say 'My turn... your turn!' exaggerating each switch.

activities.title: Cooking Turns
activities.desc: While cooking together, take turns: 'You stir... now my turn!' Real-life turn-taking is the best practice.

social.text: Notices other children and joins them to play

activities.title: Playground Practice
activities.desc: Visit playgrounds and encourage them to approach other kids: 'Do you want to play with them?' Stay nearby for support.

activities.title: Play Date Host
activities.desc: Invite a friend over. Having a peer in their own space makes social interaction more comfortable.

social.text: Shows concern for a crying friend

activities.title: Empathy Coaching
activities.desc: When they see someone upset, narrate: 'They're sad. What could we do to help?' This builds empathy skills.

activities.title: Feelings Books
activities.desc: Read books about feelings. Ask 'How does the bear feel?' and 'What would you do?' This builds emotional vocabulary.

speech.text: Says sentences with 4 or more words

activities.title: Story Starters
activities.desc: Start a sentence and let them finish: 'Today I saw a...' Encourage longer answers: 'Tell me more!'

activities.title: Dinner Chat
activities.desc: At meals, ask 'What was your favorite part of today?' Model full sentences in your answer too.

speech.text: Says some words from a song, story, or nursery rhyme

activities.title: Pause & Fill
activities.desc: Sing a familiar song but pause before the last word of each line. Let them fill it in: 'Twinkle twinkle little...'

activities.title: Story Recall
activities.desc: After reading a familiar book, ask 'What happened next?' Let them retell in their own words.

speech.text: Talks about at least one thing that happened during the day

activities.title: Highs & Lows
activities.desc: At dinner or bedtime, share your high and low of the day. Then ask: 'What was YOUR favorite part?'

activities.title: Photo Review
activities.desc: Look at photos from the day on your phone together. Ask 'What were we doing here?' helps them narrate events.

speech.text: Answers simple questions like 'What is a coat for?'

activities.title: What's It For?
activities.desc: Point to everyday objects: 'What's a spoon for? What's a blanket for?' Accept creative answers too!

activities.title: Silly Wrong Answers
activities.desc: Give funny wrong answers: 'Is a hat for your feet?' They'll laugh and correct you — that's learning!

speech.text: Says sentences with 4 or more words

activities.title: Sentence Building
activities.desc: When they say 2-3 words, expand: 'Big truck!' → 'Yes, I see a BIG RED truck!' They'll start building longer sentences.

activities.title: Story Starters
activities.desc: Start a sentence and let them finish: 'Once upon a time, there was a...' They practice building longer thoughts!

speech.text: Tells you about their day using at least 2-3 sentences

activities.title: Dinner Share
activities.desc: At dinner, ask 'What was the best part of your day?' Give them time to put thoughts into multiple sentences.

activities.title: Day Drawing
activities.desc: Ask them to draw something from their day, then tell you about the picture. Drawing supports verbal storytelling!

physical.text: Catches a large ball most of the time

activities.title: Balloon Catch
activities.desc: Start with a balloon — it moves slowly! Once they catch that, upgrade to a soft ball.

activities.title: Roll & Catch
activities.desc: Sit facing each other and roll a ball. Then try gentle underhand throws. 'Ready? Here it comes!'

physical.text: Serves self food or pours water with supervision

activities.title: Pour Practice
activities.desc: Use a small pitcher with a little water. Let them pour into a cup at meals. Spills are part of learning!

activities.title: Scoop Station
activities.desc: At meals, let them use a serving spoon to scoop their own food from a bowl. Independence at the table!

physical.text: Unbuttons some buttons

activities.title: Big Button Dress-Up
activities.desc: Practice with large-button shirts (like a parent's shirt). Big buttons are easier to start with!

activities.title: Button Board
activities.desc: Sew large buttons onto felt or fabric. Let them practice buttoning/unbuttoning as a fun activity.

physical.text: Holds crayon or pencil between fingers and thumb (not a fist)

activities.title: Short Crayon Trick
activities.desc: Break crayons in half. Short crayons naturally encourage a finger grip instead of a fist grip!

activities.title: Dot-to-Dot
activities.desc: Draw simple dot-to-dot pictures. Connecting dots requires the precision that builds proper pencil grip.

physical.text: Catches a large ball most of the time

activities.title: Balloon Catch
activities.desc: Start with a balloon — it moves slowly and is easy to catch. Then work up to a soft, large ball.

activities.title: Roll & Catch
activities.desc: Start by rolling the ball, then progress to gentle underhand tosses. Get close and back up as they get better!

physical.text: Pours from a small pitcher and cuts with supervision using child scissors

activities.title: Pour Practice
activities.desc: Let them pour their own water from a small pitcher at meals. Start with a little water — spills are learning!

activities.title: Cutting Practice
activities.desc: Draw thick lines on paper and let them cut along them with child scissors. This builds hand strength!

cognitive.text: Names a few colors of items

activities.title: Color Scavenger Hunt
activities.desc: 'Find something blue!' Take turns picking a color and hunting for it around the room or outside.

activities.title: Color Sort Snack
activities.desc: Sort colored snacks by color before eating: 'All the red ones here, green ones there!' Then eat!

cognitive.text: Tells what comes next in a well-known story

activities.title: Predictable Pause
activities.desc: Read a familiar story and stop before something happens: 'And then the bear...' Let them finish!

activities.title: Sequence Cards
activities.desc: Draw or print 3 pictures from a story. Mix them up and let them put them in order.

cognitive.text: Draws a person with 3 or more body parts

activities.title: Body Drawing
activities.desc: Draw a person together: 'First the head... then the body... arms... legs!' Talk through each part.

activities.title: Mirror Drawing
activities.desc: Have them look in a mirror and draw what they see. 'What do you see? Eyes! Draw eyes!'

cognitive.text: Understands and uses words for counting

activities.title: Stair Counting
activities.desc: Count each stair as you go up and down. Count snacks, toys, fingers, toes — make counting a habit!

activities.title: Snack Count
activities.desc: 'How many crackers do you want? Let's count: one... two... three!' They learn numbers have meaning.

cognitive.text: Names a few colors of items

activities.title: Color Hunt
activities.desc: Go on a color hunt: 'Can you find something RED?' Visit each room and find that color. Great for learning color names!

activities.title: Sorting Colors
activities.desc: Sort toys, snacks, or crayons by color. Name each color as you sort: 'All the blue ones go here!'

cognitive.text: Counts to 5 or higher

activities.title: Count Everything
activities.desc: Count stairs as you climb, count grapes on the plate, count cars in the parking lot. Every moment is a counting opportunity!

activities.title: Number Hunt
activities.desc: Find numbers in the environment — on signs, clocks, addresses. 'Look! There's a 3!' Makes numbers real.

social.text: Pretends to be something else during play (teacher, superhero)

activities.title: Dress-Up Box
activities.desc: Keep a box of hats, capes, and scarves. Ask 'Who are you today?' Play along with their character!

activities.title: Pretend Restaurant
activities.desc: Set up a pretend restaurant. They're the chef/waiter. You order, they 'cook' and serve. Builds imagination!

social.text: Asks to play with other children

activities.title: Practice Words
activities.desc: Practice at home: 'Can I play with you?' Role-play the scenario. Having the words ready builds confidence.

activities.title: Playground Buddy
activities.desc: At the park, stay nearby as they approach other kids. Coach gently: 'Ask them if you can play!'

social.text: Comforts others who are hurt or sad

activities.title: Check-In Practice
activities.desc: When someone is upset, model: 'Are you okay? Do you need a hug?' Then let your child try.

activities.title: Teddy Doctor
activities.desc: Pretend teddy is hurt. 'Oh no, teddy fell! What should we do?' They practice caring and comforting.

social.text: Avoids danger (doesn't jump from tall heights)

activities.title: Safety Talks
activities.desc: Casually talk about why: 'We don't climb on that because we could fall and get hurt.' Understanding 'why' helps.

activities.title: Safe/Not Safe Game
activities.desc: Point to things and ask: 'Is it safe to jump from there?' Let them think through the answer.

social.text: Changes behavior based on where they are (library vs playground)

activities.title: Where Are We?
activities.desc: Before entering a new place, ask: 'We're at the library. What voice do we use here?' Prep helps!

activities.title: Inside/Outside Voice
activities.desc: Practice switching between 'inside voice' (quiet) and 'outside voice' (loud). Make it a fun game!

social.text: Likes to be a 'helper' and wants to help with tasks

activities.title: Real Helping
activities.desc: Give them real tasks — setting the table, sorting laundry, wiping surfaces. Real jobs build confidence and responsibility!

activities.title: Helper of the Day
activities.desc: Create a 'helper of the day' routine with a special task. They'll love the responsibility and recognition!

speech.text: Tells a story with at least 2 events

activities.title: Story Chain
activities.desc: Start a story: 'Once upon a time, a dog ran outside...' Ask: 'Then what happened?' Let them add the next part!

activities.title: Day Recap
activities.desc: At bedtime, ask 'Tell me two things you did today.' Help them connect events: 'First... then...'

speech.text: Answers simple questions about a book or story

activities.title: Story Questions
activities.desc: After reading, ask: 'Who was in the story? What happened? Why was she sad?' Start with 'who' and 'what.'

activities.title: Movie Chat
activities.desc: After watching a show together: 'Who was your favorite character? What happened at the end?'

speech.text: Keeps a conversation going with 3 or more back-and-forth exchanges

activities.title: Interview Time
activities.desc: Pretend to interview them: 'What's your favorite food? Why? What do you like about it?' Keep going!

activities.title: Car Conversations
activities.desc: Use car rides for longer chats. 'What if we could fly? Where would you go?' Open questions keep it going.

speech.text: Uses or recognizes simple rhymes

activities.title: Rhyme Time
activities.desc: Say a word and find rhymes: 'Cat... bat... hat...' Take turns adding one. How many can you find?

activities.title: Silly Rhyme Songs
activities.desc: Sing songs with rhymes (Down by the Bay, etc). Pause before the rhyming word and let them fill it in!

speech.text: Tells a simple story using full sentences

activities.title: Story Chain
activities.desc: Start a story: 'Once there was a dog...' and take turns adding sentences. They practice narrative structure!

activities.title: Picture Story
activities.desc: Give them 3-4 pictures and ask them to put them in order and tell the story. 'First... then... and then...'

speech.text: Uses future tense ('Grandma will be here tomorrow')

activities.title: Tomorrow Talk
activities.desc: Talk about upcoming plans: 'Tomorrow we WILL go to the park.' Ask them: 'What WILL we do there?'

activities.title: Plan Together
activities.desc: Let them help plan activities: 'What will we make for lunch?' Using 'will' and 'going to' practices future tense.

physical.text: Buttons some buttons

activities.title: Getting Dressed Race
activities.desc: Time how fast they can button their shirt. Try to beat their own record. No competing — just personal bests!

activities.title: Doll Dress-Up
activities.desc: Practice buttoning on a doll or stuffed animal's clothes. Easier to see and manipulate smaller buttons.

physical.text: Hops on one foot

activities.title: Hopscotch
activities.desc: Draw a simple hopscotch with chalk. Practice hopping on one foot through the squares!

activities.title: Flamingo Game
activities.desc: 'Let's be flamingos!' Stand on one foot and see who can hold it longest. Then try hopping!

physical.text: Uses a fork and spoon properly

activities.title: Restaurant Night
activities.desc: Set the table fancy and practice 'restaurant manners' — using fork in one hand, spoon in the other.

activities.title: Utensil Practice
activities.desc: Serve foods that need both utensils: soup needs a spoon, pasta needs a fork. Practice with purpose!

physical.text: Can dress and undress without much help

activities.title: Outfit Picker
activities.desc: Let them pick tomorrow's outfit and practice putting it on the night before. Independence + excitement!

activities.title: Timer Challenge
activities.desc: Set a gentle timer and see if they can get dressed before it goes off. Make it fun, not stressful!

physical.text: Buttons some buttons and might tie shoes with help

activities.title: Button Board
activities.desc: Practice buttoning on a large-button shirt or a DIY button board. Start with large buttons and work down to smaller ones.

activities.title: Lacing Cards
activities.desc: Use lacing cards or an old shoe to practice the lacing motion. Tie a big knot at the end so the lace doesn't pull through.

physical.text: Swings on a swing and pumps legs independently

activities.title: Pump Practice
activities.desc: Teach them 'kick out' on the forward swing and 'tuck in' on the back. It takes practice, so start with gentle pushes.

activities.title: Rhythm Swinging
activities.desc: Sing a song while they swing and cue them to kick/tuck on the beat. Adding rhythm helps them learn the pattern!

cognitive.text: Counts to 10

activities.title: Count Everything
activities.desc: Count stairs, snacks, toys, birds outside. The more they count real things, the more numbers make sense.

activities.title: Hide & Count
activities.desc: Play hide and seek — they count to 10 while you hide. Meaningful counting practice with a fun reward!

cognitive.text: Names some numbers between 1 and 5 when pointed to

activities.title: Number Hunt
activities.desc: Find numbers in the real world: on signs, clocks, books, addresses. 'What number is that?'

activities.title: Number Magnets
activities.desc: Put number magnets on the fridge. Point to one: 'What number is this?' Start with 1-5.

cognitive.text: Uses words about time (yesterday, tomorrow, morning)

activities.title: Time Talk
activities.desc: Narrate time throughout the day: 'Tomorrow we're going to the park! Yesterday we had pizza.' Model the words.

activities.title: Morning/Night Sort
activities.desc: 'Do we eat breakfast in the morning or at night? Do we put on pajamas in the morning or at night?' Quiz them!

cognitive.text: Pays attention for 5-10 minutes during activities

activities.title: Puzzle Time
activities.desc: Do a 12-24 piece puzzle together. Sit with them and work on it. Your presence helps them focus longer.

activities.title: Story Without Pictures
activities.desc: Tell a story without a book — just your voice. 'Once upon a time...' builds listening attention.

cognitive.text: Writes some letters in their name

activities.title: Name Tracing
activities.desc: Write their name in large dotted letters. Let them trace over the dots. Start with just the first letter!

activities.title: Sand Writing
activities.desc: Pour salt or sand in a tray. Let them practice writing letters with their finger. Erase by shaking — try again!

cognitive.text: Writes some letters from their name

activities.title: Name Writing
activities.desc: Write their name in dots and let them trace over it. Then try without dots. Start with just the first letter!

activities.title: Sensory Letters
activities.desc: Write letters in sand, shaving cream, or finger paint. Multi-sensory practice helps letter formation stick!

social.text: Follows rules or takes turns in games

activities.title: Simple Board Games
activities.desc: Play simple games like Candy Land or Hi-Ho Cherry-O. They practice waiting, rules, and turn-taking.

activities.title: Card Games
activities.desc: Play Go Fish or Memory. Simple rules, clear turns. Great for rule-following and patience!

social.text: Sings, dances, or acts for you

activities.title: Living Room Concert
activities.desc: Set up a 'stage' (a blanket on the floor). Let them perform! Be an enthusiastic audience.

activities.title: Dance Party
activities.desc: Put on music and dance together. Take turns showing off your best moves. They love performing!

social.text: Does simple chores at home

activities.title: Chore Chart
activities.desc: Make a simple picture chart: put away toys, wipe the table, feed the pet. Sticker for each one done!

activities.title: Helper of the Day
activities.desc: Make them the 'special helper' — setting the table, sorting laundry, watering plants. They love the title!

social.text: Is aware of gender differences

activities.title: Family Talk
activities.desc: Answer their questions simply and honestly when they notice differences. 'Boys and girls can both do anything!'

activities.title: Diverse Books
activities.desc: Read books showing all kinds of families and people. Representation builds understanding naturally.

social.text: Can tell what is real and what is make-believe

activities.title: Real or Pretend?
activities.desc: While reading or watching TV: 'Is that real or pretend?' Start obvious (dragons = pretend) and get trickier.

activities.title: Imagination Games
activities.desc: Play pretend together and then talk about it: 'That was fun pretending! In real life, can we fly?' They learn the difference.

social.text: Follows rules in simple board games or card games

activities.title: Game Night
activities.desc: Play simple games like Candy Land, Go Fish, or Memory. Focus on taking turns, following rules, and being a good sport.

activities.title: Win & Lose Practice
activities.desc: Let them win sometimes and lose sometimes. Practice saying 'good game' either way. This builds emotional regulation.

badges.description: Check ALL milestones across ALL areas

badges.name: Growth Tracker
badges.description: Come back for a second check-in

badges.name: Leap Forward
badges.description: Improve your score from a previous check

badges.name: Goal Getter
badges.description: Achieve a milestone goal you set
